narration: A heap keeps one very important item easy to reach at the top.
callout: In this min-heap picture, the smallest value is on top.

narration: A min-heap keeps the smallest on top. A max-heap keeps the largest on top.
decision: Choose the heap type based on what item you need easiest to reach.

predictionPrompt.prompt: If you keep the current 5 lowest costs and want to remove the biggest of those survivors when a cheaper cost appears, which heap fits best?

choices.label: Max-heap
choices.feedback: Correct. The biggest of the current best costs should be easy to remove.

choices.label: Min-heap
choices.feedback: Not this one. A min-heap would make the smallest survivor easiest to remove.

narration: A heap is not fully sorted. It mainly promises something special about the top item.
callout: Top ready does not mean everything else is perfectly ordered.

reflectionPrompt.prompt: Why can a heap still be useful even if every value is not fully sorted?
